114742.explanation: We will work on new forms of participation. In doing so, the municipality will make clear agreements in advance about what residents can decide themselves, what the municipality will implement, and what is not possible.

114745.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gouda initiated in 2022 an adjustment to the retail hours regulation to allow this for shops. Retailers themselves can best estimate when they want to be open.

114748.explanation: Gouda is a city where refugees are welcome, where you are free to be who you are and where everyone can participate. In Gouda, we already invest extra through the Goudse Plus, providing a warm welcome and a good landing for new city residents.

114752.explanation: Gouda enforces the fireworks ban; we celebrate New Year together. Fireworks cause much damage, injury, and nuisance annually to people, animals, and emergency workers. Together with residents, entrepreneurs, and associations, we organize a central, festive countdown event where the entire city comes together.

114757.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers good local journalism important. This is only possible if the local broadcaster can produce quality programs so that council members, aldermen, and the mayor can be critically questioned about policy and there is enough room for music programs.

114759.explanation: We make sports and culture more accessible to everyone.

114763.explanation: We remain cautious about the use of cameras in public spaces. The municipality regularly checks whether a camera can be removed. Camera surveillance exists solely to enforce public order. We do not allow cameras to structurally collect personal data such as faces.

114764.explanation: We want more visible enforcement on the streets and invest in more neighborhood officers and community enforcement officers. They are the eyes and ears of a neighborhood, both day and night. Additionally, we want increased police deployment for investigating and enforcing (digital) crime.

114766.explanation: During the revision of the housing ordinance, GroenLinks-PvdA Gouda made it possible for housing seekers with local ties to receive priority when seeking a home.

114768.explanation: People who buy a house must live in it themselves and not rent it out for profit or resell quickly. This keeps homes available for those who truly seek a place to live.

114771.explanation: We ensure that work pays off and truly suits people. Everyone deserves a job that matches their talent, education, experience, and personal situation. Every contribution to our society is valuable; from volunteering to full-time employment.

114773.explanation: We put people first, not the system. We do not start with rules, but with what someone needs to move forward. Therefore, the municipality works with fixed contact persons, fewer forms, and tailored solutions.

114776.explanation: We continue to prioritize traffic safety, especially for pedestrians and cyclists. The principle is that all car roads within the city become 30 km/h zones. In streets, near schools, and in the city center, we reduce the speed to 15 km/h.

114778.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gouda supports appropriate help for everyone.

114779.explanation: The municipality’s purchasing policy always takes care workers' salaries into account. To reduce waiting lists, the available budget is distributed as well as possible among light, short-term needs and the most complex problems.

114780.explanation: Necessary medical aids are reimbursed. No one in Gouda should depend on income to receive the care they need. We will also continue to provide free menstrual products at central locations in the city, such as the library and community centers.

114781.explanation: Gouda is in a sound financial position and we want to keep it that way. We pursue a careful financial policy and critically review the municipality’s own expenditures. This ensures we have room for investments in affordable housing, green neighborhoods, and social security even in uncertain times.

114782.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has consistently chosen to keep the dog tax, based on the principle that the polluter pays. This amount should not be very high. The proceeds from the dog tax are used, for example, to place enough dog waste bins and to deploy extra inspectors.

114784.explanation: Gouda is a compact city where space is scarce. Yet we must move forward. More housing is needed, we want to generate more sustainable energy and create space for children to play safely, water, trees, plants, and animals.

114785.explanation: In 2022, GroenLinks-PvdA Gouda took the initiative to amend the shop opening hours regulation to make this possible for stores.

114786.explanation: We prevent people from the asylum seeker center (azc) from ending up on the street. Therefore, we continue to invest in their shelter and access to care. For status holders, we quickly arrange suitable housing.

114788.explanation: There is hardly any agricultural land in Gouda, and regionally we look at where it is appropriate to allow renewable energy generation, such as wind turbines and solar panels. GroenLinks-PvdA mainly focuses on home insulation and, for Gouda, on solar panels on roofs.

114790.explanation: We invest in welfare work, community centers, and libraries. Meeting centers like the Nelson Mandela Center, De Walvis, and Van Noord can keep their doors open more often and for longer.

114792.explanation: Coffeeshops attract customers from across the city and beyond. This causes (traffic) nuisance for local residents. We engage in good consultations with owners of coffeeshops in the city center to conduct research into relocating them to the city outskirts.

114797.explanation: We deliberately keep the cost for a first parking permit low. The rates for visitors and for second or third car parking permits will be significantly higher. People who park briefly, such as suppliers, (maternity) care providers, and informal caregivers, can still park nearby.

114798.explanation: Cars take up too much space. Therefore, we make the city center low-traffic and quickly introduce regulated parking in more locations. Fewer cars mean cleaner air and more space for greenery, walking, cycling, and playing.

114799.explanation: Cars take up too much space. Therefore, we make the city center low-traffic and quickly introduce regulated parking in more places. A car-free city center is not practical because there are residents there who have difficulty walking. We do support a low-traffic city center.

114800.explanation: The municipality makes agreements with the polluting industries in Gouda to reduce emissions. Our goal to be CO2-neutral by 2040 and to improve air quality in Gouda guides us. In the long term, there will be no room for polluting industries in Gouda.

114801.explanation: Ideally, trucks may only drive on the Koningin Wilhelminaweg as destination traffic. We limit the consequences of soil subsidence. We identify foundation risks and work together with other authorities and residents on how to prevent or postpone foundation damage.

114803.explanation: Gouda's identity is connected to the water in and around the city: the Gouwe, the Hollandse IJssel, and the canals.

114742.explanation: Citizen participation is of great importance. D66 supports the binding corrective referendum. That is only a small part of the larger picture of participation.

114745.explanation: As far as public order allows, entrepreneurs should be able to decide their own opening hours.

114748.explanation: The asylum seeker center functions very well, especially thanks to the Goudse Plus approach we use in Gouda.

114752.explanation: There is room for an extra event on New Year's Eve. It does not have to be organized by the municipality, nor does it have to be fireworks.

114757.explanation: The regional broadcaster receives subsidies from provincial funds. We do not provide any further structural subsidies.

114759.explanation: First and foremost, this is the responsibility of parents. In the context of equal opportunities, it is possible to cover this from poverty schemes.

114763.explanation: Camera surveillance can temporarily provide a solution in some cases.

114764.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with police officers.

114766.explanation: As much as possible, social rental homes should benefit housing seekers from Gouda.

114768.explanation: We maintain the current owner-occupancy regulation.

114771.explanation: We want everyone to participate, which can happen in many ways; only when everyone can participate does everyone truly feel at home.

114773.explanation: The border of Gouda is already very generous compared to other municipalities.

114776.explanation: We emphasize cycling, walking, and public transport. Where possible, the maximum speed limit will be 30 km/h. This applies to all residential neighborhoods; main roads within built-up areas remain at 50 km/h.

114778.explanation: There is a scarcity of care providers. Quality of care is more important than the provider's belief system.

114779.explanation: The decision framework contains quality requirements and a desire to pay a responsible cost price. Price is important but not leading.

114780.explanation: There are initiatives in the city that provide this; expansion does not seem necessary.

114781.explanation: Property tax (ozb) may not rise faster than inflation. There is no room for reduction.

114782.explanation: We maintain the dog tax. We also incur structural costs to realize and maintain facilities for dogs.

114784.explanation: Existing business parks are important. We reserve space for entrepreneurs from Gouda. There is no space within Gouda; we focus on regional developments.

114785.explanation: We stand for a liberal shop opening hours policy. Entrepreneurs determine their own opening times and days as much as possible.

114786.explanation: We must continue to be able to give priority to promote flow from the asylum seeker centers.

114788.explanation: We have no agricultural land in Gouda...

114790.explanation: There already is one. Additionally, there is a neighborhood center in Oosterwei, which is also available for Goverwelle.

114792.explanation: Especially outside the city center, the establishment of a well-accessible coffeeshop is desirable. Relocating one of the coffeeshops from the city center positively impacts traffic safety.

114797.explanation: We mainly need more supportive policies. Better visitor regulations, better facilities around the city center, more shared mobility.

114798.explanation: The number does not necessarily need to decrease; it is not an objective in itself.

114799.explanation: We want a low-traffic city center. Residents must still be able to reach their homes.

114800.explanation: Unfeasible, unaffordable, undesirable. This is an absolute pipe dream. The Cargill factory is relatively green, a major employer, and relocating a well-functioning company is unaffordable.

114801.explanation: For the time being, the KW road is still indispensable as an access and transport route. We improve its layout and reduce the speed, as described in the traffic circulation plan.

114803.explanation: The city center is only very limitedly navigable by boat. Where possible, we maintain or improve navigability. It is an illusion that the entire inner city can be made navigable.

114742.explanation: ChristenUnie does not consider a referendum a good tool for participation. We choose broad participation where residents can also provide arguments.

114745.explanation: The economy is not only about growth and consumption, but also about quality of life and the balance between working, caring, and resting. We see Sunday as a collective moment of rest. Therefore, we do not support extended Sunday opening hours for shops or contractual obligations to be open.

114748.explanation: The municipality takes responsibility for housing asylum seekers and abides by the dispersal law.

114752.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that organizing a fireworks show is not the task of the municipality. It is up to residents to take the initiative for this.

114757.explanation: ChristenUnie stands for strengthening the local broadcaster, as a unifying factor and for local journalism.

114759.explanation: All children in Gouda get the chance through the Broad School to play sports, learn to play a musical instrument, and come into contact with art and culture. Children from families in poverty can participate free or at a discount via the Rotterdam Pass.

114763.explanation: Use of cameras for prevention and enforcement is seen as a good option, provided the privacy of our residents is guaranteed.

114764.explanation: ChristenUnie strongly supports the safety of enforcement officers (BOAs), but prefers proven measures that effectively improve their feeling of safety.

114766.explanation: ChristenUnie stands for priority for Gouda housing seekers.

114768.explanation: We already have a self-occupancy obligation in Gouda, which we have agreed to.

114771.explanation: Everyone has been given talents to contribute to society. Even those who can no longer work often want to mean something. ChristenUnie recognizes this commitment and wants to create space for people to contribute according to their abilities-through volunteer work, informal care, or other forms of involvement.

114773.explanation: ChristenUnie stands for continuing the Policy Framework on Poverty and Debt 2025-2029 – Breaking the Cycle. The threshold is set at 130 percent of the income limit, which is already high compared to other municipalities.

114776.explanation: The higher the speed, the greater the risk of injury. In Gouda, work is underway to reduce the maximum speed to 30 km/h, except on roads with public transport and main connecting roads.

114778.explanation: ChristenUnie finds it important that support under the Social Support Act (Wmo) remains available to the truly vulnerable and that public funds are used as effectively as possible. We choose quality and freedom of choice in this.

114779.explanation: ChristenUnie finds it important that support from youth care remains available to the truly vulnerable and that public funds are used as effectively as possible. We choose quality and freedom of choice in this.

114780.explanation: ChristenUnie is against the free distribution of menstrual products but strongly supports help for poverty. We focus on early detection, financial self-reliance, and support for families, children, and the elderly. We provide targeted help and tailor-made solutions.

114781.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that the local tax burden, including local levies, may only increase by a maximum of inflation. The costs for residents and entrepreneurs remain as low as possible.

114782.explanation: The dog tax can be abolished because it is not efficient.

114784.explanation: ChristenUnie wants to invest in the modernization and revitalization of existing business parks.

114785.explanation: We are not in favor of further loosening of Sunday opening hours of shops or contractual obligations for entrepreneurs to be open on Sundays.

114786.explanation: The municipality continues to take responsibility for housing asylum seekers with a residence status and commits to finding suitable housing.

114790.explanation: This neighborhood deserves a better community center and an upgrade of Goverwelle shopping center.

114792.explanation: ChristenUnie does not want to normalize drug use but to combat it. Therefore, we do not want new coffeeshops.

114797.explanation: There should be a regulation for resident visitors in the city center. The rate for street parking does not need to be lowered.

114798.explanation: There must be a parking plan for the entire city, in consultation with residents and entrepreneurs. It will describe how sufficient parking spaces at the right locations will be realized.

114799.explanation: The city center will be low-traffic: we want fewer parked cars along the canals and on the streets. This is not the same as car-free because the city must remain accessible.

114800.explanation: The municipality cannot just decide on this: Cargill is the landowner and has permits and rights. Buying out costs more money than Gouda has.

114801.explanation: The Queen Wilhelmina Road will be redesigned. After that, the road will preferably be for destination traffic only.

114803.explanation: We would like an ‘Intimate boating route’ through the city center to better experience Gouda from the water.

114742.explanation: We are in favor of a citizens' assembly, so involve citizens more at the start. A referendum can be useful for topics that can simply be answered with yes or no.

114748.explanation: We are against large-scale shelter but closing is too far for us given the issues in the asylum chain. We prefer mixed use of the large COA shelters, also for emergency seekers.

114752.explanation: Not mandatory, but allowed. Laser show has been tried. Now that a nationwide ban will come into effect, we will see if we can find financial room for this.

114757.explanation: If the local broadcaster disappears, then we will consider doing so.

114759.explanation: There are very good teaching methods available like 1-2-3 Sing which teachers can use. The curriculum is quite full, and it depends on school policy whether space is made. It can be done but also depends on teachers' interest. In short, it's not a matter of money but making choices and utilizing what is already available. Also at the broad school.

114763.explanation: Yes. See also problems at Bloemendaal shopping center. It is becoming increasingly necessary.

114764.explanation: Given the increasing aggression in society towards people in roles such as community enforcement officers, police, and emergency responders, we support allowing the visible carrying of batons. The approach remains de-escalation and not their use. The choice can possibly be left to the enforcement officer.

114766.explanation: It is desirable, but not always possible.

114768.explanation: By maintaining this, you ensure that private individuals can no longer buy, renovate, and rent out properties. Part of the rental market (middle segment) disappears. In short, all such measures backfire and do not solve the housing shortage problem.

114773.explanation: This is already very high in Gouda.

114779.explanation: Price is important, but above all quality must be measured.

114781.explanation: Include this in the total housing costs and these must go down. Gouda is extremely expensive.

114784.explanation: Especially focus on improving/greening the current sites.

114785.explanation: Fine as it is. We do need to see if sufficient people come on shopping Sundays.

114786.explanation: The municipality has the right to allocate part of the social housing stock. That seems like priority but it is not. There is an obligation to house status holders. We are lagging behind on this.

114788.explanation: In a dense and compact city like Gouda, we encourage solar panels on roofs of businesses and houses. Do not sacrifice costly green spaces for solar panels.

114790.explanation: In principle, a community center in every neighborhood.

114792.explanation: We want to keep the current number. However, relocate some from the city center due to car traffic.

114797.explanation: It is now unclear why there is such a large difference between the rates. These are also not substantiated.

114798.explanation: Car ownership continues to grow, so this is not a good solution.

114799.explanation: Nice idea, but then with more parking spaces on the edges of the city center. Car-reduced can only be realized after that. Car-free is a much bigger step and not recommended because many people live and lead their lives in the city center as well.

114801.explanation: If that is necessary for safety, and also possible, it is worth considering.

114803.explanation: No, that is far too expensive and yields nothing for the city.

114742.explanation: The aim must be to involve residents in advance in plans so they can think along about their own living environment. The municipal council is there for a complete balancing of interests, such a balancing does not come across well in a referendum.

114745.explanation: Yes, these businesses should have the free choice when they are open or not. The only caveat is that the municipality sets the interests of local residents and public order and safety as preconditions.

114748.explanation: Gouda must comply with the mandate to accommodate asylum seekers as stated in the distribution law. However, VVD Gouda opposes large-scale accommodation and sees the high influx of asylum seekers in the Netherlands as a problem. When people are here, they should be accommodated humanely.

114752.explanation: It costs the municipality (and thus the residents) a lot of money to organize a fireworks show for a limited target group. There should be room for initiatives from society/entrepreneurs.

114757.explanation: The municipality already contributes to a local broadcaster. Extra money is not an end in itself. When a local broadcaster has good plans, discussions can always be held.

114759.explanation: No, music lessons are essentially cultural education and therefore the responsibility of the parents of primary school children.

114763.explanation: Yes, camera surveillance can be used as an extension of street services, allowing crime to be better detected and/or proven.

114764.explanation: Yes, community enforcement officers increasingly face situations where they deal with serious violence. The baton can provide more defensive options and act as a deterrent. Strict requirements and training must apply.

114766.explanation: We see that Gouda is an attractive city. This means many home seekers come to Gouda. As a result, residents who have built their social network here find it difficult to get a home in Gouda. Therefore, priority for them is important.

114768.explanation: People who have acquired ownership of a home have the right to dispose of it. For example, with this statement, parents buying a home for their children would no longer be able to do so.

114771.explanation: Social assistance is a social provision. We are here for those who need it. We can expect that someone contributes according to their ability. The reciprocal obligation is our standard; those who can contribute, contribute.

114773.explanation: We will stop using our own definition of poverty and follow the Nibud. So the threshold should not be raised any further. Only when income, including all poverty arrangements someone is entitled to, falls below the poverty line is there poverty.

114776.explanation: On through roads, 50 km/h can be used (and remain) when, for example, separated traffic flows are applied. In many places, flow is not compromised at 30 km/h, so this can be applied there.

114778.explanation: Social provisions are for everyone, with care appropriate to the person or situation. It is not the government's task to create that care offer, but it is to protect freedom of choice.

114779.explanation: In purchasing care services, criteria such as quality, customer satisfaction, (no) waiting lists, and problem-solving capacity are central. Good intake and appropriate care are ultimately better and more affordable.

114780.explanation: When people cannot afford tampons or sanitary pads due to poverty, the solution is an integral part of poverty arrangements. Free distribution of tampons and sanitary pads is not a proportional measure.

114781.explanation: Local levies must be lowered so that residents of Gouda can determine themselves how they spend their money.

114782.explanation: The dog tax is still a tax that yields little and is also unfair.

114784.explanation: There simply is no room for that.

114785.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves whether they want to be open on Sundays or not. In Gouda, we no longer have shopping Sundays, but entrepreneurs are always allowed to open on Sundays. We want to keep it that way.

114786.explanation: Many residents of Gouda have to search for a home for years. It is therefore very unfair if this target group is given priority.

114788.explanation: For the VVD Gouda, it is essential to have a reliable energy supply. The starting point is solar panels on roofs. As a temporary measure to deal with grid congestion, solar fields are negotiable.

114790.explanation: Community centers have a social function. In Goverwelle we already have 't Govertje. An additional community center therefore adds no value.

114792.explanation: The coffeeshops (and their visitors) cause structural nuisance and damage the city's image; traffic nuisance, intimidation, and a decreased sense of safety. Therefore, closure is always a real option, not a last resort. The number of coffeeshops will not increase.

114797.explanation: Paid parking in the surrounding neighborhoods must cover its costs. This disproportionately disadvantages inner-city residents. Entrepreneurs also lose customers. Therefore, lowering parking fees is also possible in the city center.

114798.explanation: Car ownership is increasing; it would be very unwise to reduce the number of parking spaces. People need a car, so ensure they can park.

114799.explanation: Car-free and low-car are not the same. A low-car city center is possible under certain conditions; good logistics and an attractive stay climate. Car-free is unfeasible and would be devastating for the business climate.

114800.explanation: When designing a neighborhood, you would not place a factory in this location. However, it has been here for a long time, provides a lot of employment, and relocating it would be extremely costly.

114801.explanation: With a number of companies located around Koningin Wilhelminaweg, it is unfeasible to impose a truck ban. The accessibility of the west and south sides of Gouda is already vulnerable. This measure would make Gouda inaccessible.

114803.explanation: A navigable city center is a nice goal, but not at any cost. We look at whether we can combine works, but we also consider whether the spending of public funds is appropriate.

114742.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Gouda believes that residents must be seriously involved in important decisions. Instruments like referenda can increase support, provided it is clear what is being decided and what happens with the outcome.

114745.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space, but opening hours also require coordination regarding livability and safety. Gemeentebelangen Gouda chooses tailor-made solutions per area, in consultation with entrepreneurs and residents, not complete deregulation or strict rules.

114748.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Gouda chooses responsibility and realism. Shelter is a legal and social task. However, shelter must be temporary, manageable, and well organized, with attention to support and livability in the surroundings.

114752.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Gouda is open to alternatives around New Year's Eve, especially now that a general fireworks ban is in place. A municipal fireworks show is not a given. Choices about this require weighing costs, safety, nuisance, and support among residents.

114757.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for information and democracy. Extra support is possible, provided it is targeted and transparent, but requires careful consideration of public funds and clear agreements about independence and added value.

114759.explanation: Music and culture are important for development, but a general subsidy for every child is not self-evident. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for targeted support, so that children from low-income families can participate.

114763.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in specific problem locations but is not an end in itself. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for targeted use where necessary, with clear justification, temporary application, and attention to privacy.

114764.explanation: Safe working conditions for enforcement officers are important, but arming them requires careful consideration. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions, good training, and cooperation with the police, not a general introduction of batons.

114766.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants to build for people connected to Gouda, but within legal frameworks. Local priority where permitted, without exclusion or division, with attention to vulnerable groups and regional agreements.

114768.explanation: The housing market is meant for living, not speculation. Gemeentebelangen Gouda supports owner-occupancy obligations to keep homes available for residents and first-time buyers and to counteract price increases.

114771.explanation: Participation is important, but mandatory work must not be coercion or punishment. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions and activation according to ability, with guidance and appropriate work, not a general obligation.

114773.explanation: More and more people with low to middle incomes are experiencing financial difficulties. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants assistance to better match reality, so that support is also accessible for workers who just fall outside current limits.

114776.explanation: 30 km/h can increase safety, but not every road is suitable for this. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions per street and neighborhood, based on safety, design, and support, not a generic reduction.

114778.explanation: Freedom of choice and appropriate care are important, but 'always' is not realistic. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants care to align as much as possible with someone's background, within what is practical and organizationally feasible.

114779.explanation: In youth care, quality must come first. Gemeentebelangen Gouda believes that cost should never take precedence over expertise and effectiveness. Cheap care that does not work leads to bigger problems and higher costs later.

114780.explanation: Period poverty must not be a barrier to participation. Gemeentebelangen Gouda supports free provision in accessible locations, as part of poverty policy and equal opportunities for everyone.

114781.explanation: Gouda has relatively high housing costs. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants to reduce these costs and believes property tax (ozb) may only rise in line with inflation, so that living remains affordable for residents and entrepreneurs.

114782.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair increase in charges without a clear return. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants to lower local charges and chooses a clean public space via good policy and enforcement, not through a separate tax.

114784.explanation: Employment is important, but space is scarce. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for careful management of space: first better use and sustainability of existing business parks, expansion only if really necessary and appropriate.

114785.explanation: Shopping Sundays require a balance between entrepreneurs' interests, freedom of choice, and moments of rest. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions and consultation with entrepreneurs and residents, not standard expansion or limitation of shopping Sundays.

114786.explanation: Gemeentebelangen Gouda recognizes legal obligations but wants no distortions in the housing market. Priority is possible where necessary, within the law and considering support, scarcity, and equal treatment of home seekers.

114788.explanation: Solar energy is important, but agricultural land is scarce. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for careful tailor-made solutions: first use roofs and disturbed locations and only in exceptional cases (limited) agricultural land, with clear conditions and local support.

114790.explanation: Community centers are important for meeting and cohesion, but new construction is not a goal in itself. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions: first look at the use and strengthening of existing facilities, in consultation with residents.

114792.explanation: Coffee shop policy requires careful consideration. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for control, safety, and livability. New establishments are only negotiable if demonstrably contributing to control, dispersion, and support in the area.

114797.explanation: Affordable parking keeps the center accessible for residents and visitors. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants to make parking fees fairer and better substantiated, so the center remains lively and accessible.

114798.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is already high. Gemeentebelangen Gouda believes reducing parking spaces harms livability. Mobility policy requires tailor-made and realistic choices, not a generic reduction.

114799.explanation: A livable center is important, but fully car-free goes too far. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions: good accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors, with targeted low-car measures where they work (with support).

114800.explanation: The establishment of Cargill requires careful consideration between employment, livability, and the environment. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for consultation and tailor-made solutions. Relocation is not a goal in itself but only with realistic alternatives.

114801.explanation: Traffic safety and livability are important, but a total ban requires careful consideration. Gemeentebelangen Gouda opts for tailor-made solutions: ban where necessary, with clear alternative routes and attention to business accessibility.

114803.explanation: Good navigability is important for recreation, tourism, and the use of the city canals. Gemeentebelangen Gouda wants bridges and waterways to be well maintained and, where possible, remain navigable, with attention to safety and heritage.

114742.explanation: A referendum is expensive, often has low turnout, and risks oversimplifying complex issues. CDA Gouda wants to actively involve residents in decision-making on important matters, but not through a referendum.

114745.explanation: According to the CDA, Gouda currently has good opening and closing times.

114748.explanation: Everyone wants to stop the enormous chaos in asylum reception. Closing the asylum seekers’ center (azc) would increase that chaos. Additionally, Gouda’s 'Everyone participates' approach has proven very successful.

114752.explanation: Organizing events and shows is not the municipality's task. However, the CDA believes the municipality can support initiatives from the community by granting permits and providing subsidies.

114757.explanation: The local broadcaster plays an important role in connecting Gouda residents and events in the city.

114759.explanation: CDA Gouda finds it going too far to subsidize music lessons for all children. If parents cannot afford the music lessons, the municipality has a good arrangement for this through the Child Package.

114763.explanation: We want a safe city, and sufficient cameras help with that.

114764.explanation: We want to promote the safety of the public enforcement officers (boa's); pepper spray, batons, and bodycams help with that.

114766.explanation: We find it important that residents of Gouda seeking housing can continue to live here. Therefore, CDA Gouda thinks this is a good idea.

114768.explanation: As long as it does not reduce the available housing, this is no problem for CDA Gouda. It’s different if someone buys a house to, for example, rent it out as an Airbnb. The CDA wants to counter that.

114771.explanation: This helps society. And it helps people on benefits to stay meaningfully engaged, which can contribute to finding paid employment.

114773.explanation: The income threshold currently used is good because the CDA finds it important that work pays off. Raising this threshold reduces the incentive to work, which is not the intention.

114776.explanation: The CDA considers traffic safety in neighborhoods and, for example, near schools and sports clubs very important. A 30 km/h limit is a good idea there. Access roads and main roads can remain at 50 km/h. This ensures good accessibility and smooth car traffic flow.

114778.explanation: The CDA wants to keep healthcare affordable and accessible in the future. If residents always have the right to choose their own care provider for every care need, it leads to higher costs. Therefore, CDA Gouda does not think this is a good idea.

114779.explanation: Of course, the CDA also cares about price because we want to keep care affordable and accessible. But the most important thing is that people who need care receive appropriate help quickly. That is more important than just the price.

114780.explanation: Gouda has a balanced system for poverty assistance; this can help with period poverty.

114781.explanation: The CDA is always in favor of lower taxes if possible.

114782.explanation: The revenue is used to cover the costs of the dog poop policy (such as creating dog walking areas and providing dog waste bags). This tax is necessary for that purpose.

114784.explanation: There is no room within the municipality borders for additional business parks. However, we encourage optimal usage of existing business parks.

114785.explanation: The number of shopping Sundays now seems optimal for both entrepreneurs and residents.

114786.explanation: If a refugee has a residence status, it has been established that the flight was necessary. For the flow in the asylum seekers’ center, rapid housing is necessary. Because refugees could not build rights after being registered for years, they must be given priority.

114788.explanation: Gouda itself has no suitable farmland. In the region, this could be a good idea. In Gouda, the municipality should instead focus more on rooftop solar panels.

114790.explanation: A good idea! Community centers are important meeting places for residents.

114792.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Gouda.

114797.explanation: The city center must also remain attractive for visitors by car. Therefore, parking should not become too expensive. At the same time, the CDA wants a low-car city center. Street parking should therefore not become cheaper either. However, additional parking garages and lots are needed.

114798.explanation: Public transport outside the center is less good. Residents in the neighborhoods rely more on their own car. The municipality must provide enough parking spaces. The CDA does not want to 'bully' residents by creating a scarcity of parking space.

114799.explanation: CDA Gouda supports a low-car center, not a car-free one. To keep the city center accessible for everyone.

114800.explanation: This is financially unfeasible; we are a realistic party, not dreamers. Moreover, Cargill is intertwined with Gouda. The Korte Akkeren neighborhood was originally built for workers. And Cargill still contributes significantly to employment in Gouda.

114801.explanation: This road is also used by important companies dependent on truck traffic. For the economic attractiveness of Gouda and the accessibility of our companies, the Koningin Wilhelminaweg cannot be banned for trucks.

114803.explanation: This is a romantic plan, the costs of which the CDA considers too high.

114742.explanation: We are proud of representative democracy, in which council members weigh all aspects and not just one topic.

114745.explanation: It is not healthy if cafés stay open half the night. The SGP also strongly values Sunday rest.

114748.explanation: Reducing the intake of asylum seekers is national policy. Genuine political refugees remain welcome. Gouda also has a responsibility to do its part.

114752.explanation: Disagree, this is not a task of the government.

114757.explanation: The current subsidy must remain. Let’s continue to cherish the volunteers.

114759.explanation: Although music lessons are very important and formative, this is not a primary task of the government.

114763.explanation: Nuisance, intimidation, and vandalism are major irritations for many residents. Cameras increase social safety. Citizens who behave properly have nothing to fear from cameras.

114764.explanation: The role of public enforcement officers (boa’s) is changing. Simple tasks that the police cannot get to can be taken over by boa’s. This new role also requires good training and skills.

114766.explanation: Building social rental homes costs Gouda taxpayers a lot of money. It is therefore logical that local home seekers get priority. According to the SGP, this concerns 50 percent of home seekers from Gouda.

114768.explanation: It is important to do this, given the severe housing shortage. Immediate family members are also allowed to move in.

114771.explanation: The SGP has always strongly supported such a reciprocal obligation. Moreover, people are activated and thus find it easier to return to the labor market.

114773.explanation: Income policy belongs to the national government. There are various fiscal arrangements. Increasing the income threshold locally results in people being worse off if they find a job.

114776.explanation: That is not necessary everywhere. Only in places where it is needed and where the street design has been adjusted accordingly.

114778.explanation: It is very important that people can choose identity-based care that aligns as closely as possible with their beliefs.

114779.explanation: Price is a very important criterion. At the same time, there must remain freedom of choice for identity-based care.

114780.explanation: This is not a government task. The free distribution of food is not either.

114781.explanation: Housing costs in Gouda are among the highest in the Netherlands. In recent years, the property tax has also been drastically increased. It is time for different policy!

114782.explanation: The costs of collecting and monitoring the dog tax are disproportionate to the revenue. It also serves no purpose.

114784.explanation: The demand for housing is currently higher than for business parks. At the same time, the SGP supports attracting new companies, so there must still be space for businesses.

114785.explanation: Strongly agree! The 24-hour economy makes society overstressed. One day of rest is good for everyone. From our conviction, Sunday is a day of reflection and praise.

114786.explanation: For years, the SGP has tried to change this in the Housing Regulation. It is not justifiable that status holders get priority over someone who must urgently leave home due to domestic violence.

114788.explanation: That is very unwise. Moreover, there are many roofs (homes and businesses) that are still empty.

114790.explanation: Gouda's area is such that this does not have to be in every neighborhood. That costs unnecessary government money.

114792.explanation: On the contrary. The coffeeshops in the city center should be closed. They cause a lot of nuisance including speeding cars. The SGP supports a phase-out policy for all coffeeshops in Gouda.

114797.explanation: The level of permits and street parking fees is disproportionately high. This policy is very disadvantageous for local residents and entrepreneurs.

114798.explanation: Removing parking spaces in neighborhoods leads to great frustration and only results in more emissions due to searching for parking.

114799.explanation: Car-free is very rigid. The SGP supports making the city center low-traffic, provided there are sufficient alternative parking spaces for residents.

114800.explanation: Cargill is an important employer in Gouda. There is no alternative location and relocating would also cost millions. That is unfeasible.

114801.explanation: The road must be made safer for cyclists.

114803.explanation: Gouda has turned away from the water for too long. That must change. The SGP has been committed for years to the 'intimate waterway', as well as opening the Harbor Lock. Water is indispensable to our cultural-historical city.

114742.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports direct input from residents. They want to introduce a consultative referendum for important decisions (such as major projects or reorganization) (Chapter 10.1).

114745.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants opening hours to remain part of the permit process, so that neighbors and noise disturbance are taken into account (Chapter 4.6).

114748.explanation: The Party for the Animals welcomes refugees. They believe Gouda should continue offering shelter and focus on small-scale, humane reception in the municipality, aimed at participation (Chapter 6.2).

114752.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes fireworks nuisance due to the impact on animals, health, and emergency workers. They support a ban on consumer fireworks and want no fireworks at events to protect people and animals, including no central show (Chapter 4.6).

114757.explanation: The Party for the Animals views a strong local broadcaster as the basis for a healthy democracy. Independent reporting about the city must not depend on advertising and budget cuts. (Chapter 10.1)

114759.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the municipality should invest in the broad development of children at schools, including sports, music lessons, and sustainability education (Chapter 9.1).

114763.explanation: Camera surveillance only offers an illusion of safety and does not solve the root causes of insecurity. The Party for the Animals is cautious about camera surveillance due to privacy infringement. Installing cameras is only permitted temporarily and under strict conditions (Chapter 10.3).

114764.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants fewer harsh measures (repression) and more prevention and youth work. Heavier armament of enforcement officers does not fit with this (Chapter 8.5).

114766.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes everyone has a right to housing, regardless of origin. They want priority for vulnerable groups based on necessity rather than origin to prevent exclusion (Chapter 5.1).

114768.explanation: To prevent speculation and vacancy, the Party for the Animals wants to introduce an owner-occupancy obligation. Buyers must live in the house themselves instead of only renting it out. This slows the rise of house prices (Chapter 5.1).

114771.explanation: The compulsory Participation Act is rejected. Volunteer work may be valued but not required in exchange for benefits. The individual is central (Chapters 4.9 and 4.10).

114773.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to raise the income limit to 130 percent of the social minimum for poverty schemes. This means more low-income people qualify for support, can build a small buffer, and prevents a 'poverty trap' (Chapter 4.9).

114776.explanation: The Party for the Animals focuses on traffic safety and clean air. They want a standard speed limit of 30 km/h in built-up areas (and 15 km/h in residential zones and near schools), and adjustments to road design accordingly (Chapter 7.1).

114778.explanation: The Party for the Animals values tailored care. Care providers and reception locations should consider the background, identity, and lifestyle of clients so that everyone feels safe and understood in care (Chapter 8).

114779.explanation: The Party for the Animals prioritizes quality in youth care: reducing worker overload, patient waiting lists, and providing appropriate care for youth is important, not the cheapest option (Chapter 8).

114780.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes basic needs should be accessible to everyone. Free distribution of tampons and sanitary pads fits within the party's vision (Chapter 4.9).

114781.explanation: Gouda should continue investing in a pleasant city for people, animals, and nature; lowering the property tax would undermine this care and ambitions (Chapter 10.2).

114782.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to abolish the unfair dog tax. Until then, they stipulate that proceeds are exclusively spent on dog facilities and do not disappear into the general fund (Chapter 2.3).

114784.explanation: The Party for the Animals sees more benefit in restructuring than expansion. They want to make outdated or nuisance business parks circular and clean or convert them into residential areas/green spaces, instead of creating new sites (Chapter 4.2).

114785.explanation: Party for the Animals is critical of excessive consumption and sees the city as more than just a shopping center. Peace and livability are important. Fewer shopping days align with their desire to reduce consumption and pressure on the city, but this does not necessarily have to take place on Sundays (Chapter 4.1).

114786.explanation: Party for the Animals supports the current policy of housing status holders. They want to prevent refugees from being set against other housing seekers by creating sufficient affordable housing, but do not oppose priority for this group (Chapter 6.2).

114788.explanation: Party for the Animals wants solar panels first on all roofs, then on verges, and as a last option on agricultural land. Agricultural land is meant for food production, nature, or possibly housing after downsizing livestock farming (Chapter 1.5).

114790.explanation: Party for the Animals supports more neighborhood and district centers in Gouda. These provide an important social function in neighborhoods (Chapter 8.2).

114792.explanation: Party for the Animals is open to trials with local cannabis cultivation but does not want new coffee shops near schools. Expansion of coffee shops is not a goal; environment and health come first (Chapter 8.7).

114797.explanation: Party for the Animals focuses on fewer cars, more shared mobility, and public transport. This leads to fairer use of scarce space in the city. Cheaper parking in the center does not fit with this (Chapter 7, paragraph 7.3).

114798.explanation: Through more shared cars and mobility hubs, Party for the Animals wants to free up space from parked cars for greenery, street play, and livable neighborhoods (Chapter 7.3).

114799.explanation: Party for the Animals wants to make the city center as car-free as possible. Visitors are encouraged to come by bike or public transport. Visitors with cars can use mobility hubs on the edges (Chapter 7.1).

114800.explanation: There is no room for polluting companies in Gouda, especially not on the edge of the city center. Party for the Animals prefers to transform such areas into residential neighborhoods and green spaces (Chapter 4.2).

114801.explanation: Party for the Animals does not want freight traffic through residential cores. The road is actually unsuitable for the combination of living, cycling, cars, and trucks. Trucks can also use the Nieuwe Gouwe and Industrieweg.

114803.explanation: The waterway through the center costs a lot of money and is intended to attract tourists. Party for the Animals would rather spend this money on more greenery and sustainability (Chapter 4.2).

114742.explanation: There is little trust left in politics; especially during council elections this translates into low turnout. Our democratic system is due for renewal, for example with a referendum.

114745.explanation: That should be possible and is part of free entrepreneurship. Preventing nuisance is an important point of attention.

114748.explanation: We have an agreement with COA that aligns in terms of numbers with Gouda’s obligation; however, we find the duration of the agreement too long.

114752.explanation: There was a trial with a laser show, but the number of interested people on New Year's Eve was limited. Therefore, it proved not to be an effective measure.

114757.explanation: A local broadcaster fulfills a public task, has a role in informing citizens, and is important for connection and social cohesion in the city. If extra funding is needed, it should be assessed, but the social benefit of a local broadcaster is significant.

114759.explanation: Gouda Vitaal stands for targeted support; every child should be able to have music lessons regardless of parents’ financial situation. But it is not a basic provision. By granting subsidies only when needed, it remains affordable for the municipality.

114763.explanation: Safety in the city weighs heavily. Camera surveillance can make an important contribution, even if it raises privacy concerns. It is explicitly required that the municipality strictly complies with applicable privacy legislation.

114764.explanation: A BOA (public enforcement officer) is not a police officer and does not fulfill the same tasks or role. It is mainly a symbolic measure that can lead to confusion and misunderstandings about the function of the BOA.

114766.explanation: Allocating social rental homes first to local residents (with local ties) has advantages such as promoting livability, social cohesion, and preventing displacement of vulnerable groups (starters, low incomes) due to high demand, thus keeping the city attractive.

114768.explanation: A house should be occupied by the buyer themselves rather than rented out to investors. This ensures that homes remain available and affordable for the target group they are intended for.

114771.explanation: This is too generally stated and overlooks that many people on welfare benefits are not able to do this. But when it is established that people are able, a counter-performance can be expected and it can have a positive effect on those involved.

114773.explanation: Gouda already has a high income limit (130 percent). Above that, more customization is needed to prevent poverty policy from becoming compensation for faulty policies that lead to negative income effects.

114776.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but Gouda Vitaal is not in favor of a general speed limit reduction. Gouda consists of different roads, so a generic measure does not fit. 30 km/h where needed, 50 km/h where possible.

114778.explanation: We stand for human scale and freedom of choice. Where possible, freedom of choice should be available. Care is under pressure and this wish cannot always be fulfilled. Quality, accessibility, and affordability remain leading. Freedom of choice should never mean others have to wait longer.

114779.explanation: No, a municipality must absolutely not focus only on price when selecting youth care. Municipalities are legally obliged to find a good balance between price, quality, and accessibility.

114780.explanation: Period poverty is a growing problem where people with low incomes cannot afford products, leading to missed school or workdays and discomfort; it helps alleviate financial burdens, improve hygiene, and break the taboo on menstruation.

114781.explanation: The burdens on residents and entrepreneurs have been under pressure for years. The municipality must make sharper choices and especially look at where efficiency can be improved. Lowering property tax (ozb) can be done responsibly without endangering essential services.

114782.explanation: The dog tax must be abolished. Revenues go into the municipality's general funds instead of being spent on dog policies such as dog exercise areas and dog playgrounds.

114784.explanation: Gouda Vitaal supports strengthening the local economy, but expanding industrial areas is not a goal in itself. Gouda has little space, so expansion quickly comes at the expense of livability. Expansion is only feasible on the city edges and in coordination with the region/province.

114785.explanation: Retail is already struggling; strict restrictions can shift turnover to other municipalities. At the same time, many shopping Sundays are not profitable. Fewer but better organized shopping Sundays could perhaps generate more turnover. But that choice primarily lies with the entrepreneurs themselves.

114786.explanation: No priority for status holders in social rental housing allocation. Everyone in Gouda has the right to appropriate housing. The current priority for status holders leads to longer waiting times for starters and residents with longstanding ties to the city, causing unfair distribution.

114788.explanation: In itself, there are no reasons to prohibit this, but only under strict conditions (temporary, support from residents, not at the expense of other functions).

114790.explanation: Community centers are important low-threshold meeting places in neighborhoods. They stimulate neighborhood activities, promote volunteer projects, and provide opportunities for social interaction to prevent loneliness.

114792.explanation: No expansion of coffee shops to prevent drug nuisance, crime, disturbance of public order, traffic nuisance, and social insecurity in the immediate vicinity.

114797.explanation: Paid parking in the center has become significantly more expensive in recent years and is perceived as unfair and discouraging by many residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. The center must remain affordable and accessible to visitors.

114798.explanation: The interests of residents and entrepreneurs are central. In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is already high. Removing parking spaces leads to nuisance, search traffic, and frustration among residents and visitors. Where parking spaces are removed, realistic, affordable alternatives must be offered.

114799.explanation: Our beautiful historic city center was not built for heavy traffic and many parked cars. To keep our city center livable, we focus on a low-car city center. A condition is that there is sufficient parking on the edges of the city center.

114800.explanation: Employment and a strong local economy are important. At the same time, the location is unfortunately unsuitable by today's standards. The nuisance for residents must therefore be seriously addressed. But getting Cargill to leave by the municipality will certainly cost hundreds of millions of euros.

114801.explanation: A total ban on trucks is difficult to combine with economic activities in the area. However, traffic safety and livability for residents must be heavily weighted. Targeted measures such as speed limits and time windows can be an appropriate solution.

114803.explanation: The canals and singels are an important part of Gouda's historic character. Boating through the city center should in principle be possible. However, it must remain realistic and affordable. The center is not a route for ever larger boats. It must not be at the expense of residents.

114742.explanation: JA21 wants to ensure that the voice of the population is heard not only during elections, but that residents have the chance to express themselves in the meantime about important issues. JA21 therefore advocates the introduction of binding referenda.

114745.explanation: Entrepreneurs operate according to the principle of supply and demand. If there is demand for longer opening hours, this must be possible. This creates a healthy business climate in Gouda.

114748.explanation: JA21 is against the dispersion law. Residents near the asylum reception center at Goudse Poort were not consulted. Yet the municipality decided to designate this asylum seekers center as a permanent location for 25 years. JA21 wants to reverse this decision and close this reception center as soon as possible.

114752.explanation: A fireworks show can provide a cozy finale where people come together. Of course, according to JA21, there must be support for this.

114757.explanation: The municipality must first get its core municipal tasks in order. If local broadcasters are successful, they can sustain themselves. If that is not possible, something new will naturally emerge.

114759.explanation: JA21 supports music lessons being part of the schools' curriculum. However, this is a decision for the schools themselves and not the municipality. Music lessons outside school are a task for the parents and not a municipal responsibility.

114763.explanation: In the center of Gouda, near shopping areas and beyond, more camera surveillance must be installed to combat shoplifting. JA21 believes the safety of Gouda’s residents comes first, and camera surveillance can contribute to this.

114764.explanation: BOAs responsible for safety currently only carry handcuffs to defend themselves; a baton could help, but proper training must be provided.

114766.explanation: JA21 believes that people from Gouda should have priority for social rental housing. We want them to have the opportunity to continue living in the city.

114768.explanation: Whoever buys a house is the owner of that property. It is not the municipality’s task to determine what residents do with their possessions. JA21 believes they should not be forced to live in it themselves. A residency obligation could also unnecessarily complicate renting out homes.

114771.explanation: JA21 believes that people receiving benefits should give something back to society, for example by serving coffee in a nursing home. However, this depends on the personal situation, which must always be taken into account.

114773.explanation: Gouda already applies an income threshold of 130 percent of the social welfare standard for minimum arrangements, which is above average nationally. According to JA21, further increases would make the schemes less targeted and put support for the most vulnerable under pressure. Therefore, the current threshold must remain.

114776.explanation: JA21 wants the city to remain accessible and mobile. Lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h within built-up areas leads to longer travel times towards highways and within the city. Moreover, this does not have a positive effect on emissions in Gouda.

114778.explanation: JA21 believes the municipality should offer quality care with attention to affordability. The municipality will not invest extra to contract multiple care providers to meet wishes related to religion or culture.

114779.explanation: JA21 thinks the municipality should focus on youth care that is both qualitatively good and affordable. When selecting youth care organizations, not only price but also quality and the positive effects of youth care for young people and families must be considered.

114780.explanation: JA21 thinks this is not a municipal matter; people should be able to arrange this themselves. If institutions want to offer this for free, that is their choice, but the municipality should not provide this.

114781.explanation: JA21 finds that households, shops, and business premises already face high costs. These burdens must be reduced so households and entrepreneurs have money left over each month.

114782.explanation: The dog tax is a municipal revenue model; the collected tax goes to general funds instead of serving a specific purpose. JA21 Gouda stands for tax relief, and therefore wants to abolish the dog tax as soon as possible.

114784.explanation: More industrial estates must be developed, so that both local Gouda entrepreneurs and outsiders can settle here. Gouda has limited space for these sites, making cooperation with neighboring municipalities necessary to facilitate this.

114785.explanation: JA21 believes that Sunday shopping clearly meets a demand. These days are well attended, benefit both residents and entrepreneurs, and must therefore be retained.

114786.explanation: JA21 believes status holders, like all other housing seekers, must wait their turn. Automatic priority is unfair to youth and others who sometimes wait years for a home. They too deserve a chance to build their lives.

114788.explanation: JA21 believes Gouda has limited space and must therefore make smart choices in land policy. Solar panels do not belong on agricultural land.

114790.explanation: JA21 believes Goverwelle already has a community center. This could be better utilized, but building a new one is unnecessary; improving the current community center can be combined with redevelopment of the Goverwelle neighborhood.

114792.explanation: JA21 thinks coffeeshops at their current locations cause nuisance, and Gouda already has enough coffeeshops in the city center.

114797.explanation: JA21 believes driving a car is already expensive enough. Gouda must remain easily accessible; this is only possible if parking in the city center is affordable. This keeps Gouda attractive for residents and visitors.

114798.explanation: JA21 believes that for many people driving is a basic necessity to commute to work, visit family, or go on vacation. The municipality should facilitate these residents with sufficient parking so that Gouda remains attractive to live in and visit.

114799.explanation: JA21 believes the center must remain accessible for residents by car. For many, the car is still an important basic means of transport.

114800.explanation: Cargill meets all permit requirements and also provides employment in Gouda. Measurements of benzene emissions show that Cargill is well below the legally permissible limit. JA21 therefore sees no reason to close this factory.

114801.explanation: This road is an important transit route towards Moordrecht and Rotterdam. The companies in the industrial area near Wilhelminaweg depend on this road for good traffic flow. Therefore, JA21 wants to keep this road open for truck traffic.

114803.explanation: JA21 believes boats can navigate in the city center. People can contact the bridge operator who will open the bridges for them at the agreed time.

114742.explanation: For topics that have large consequences but have not been addressed previously in elections, there will be a public consultation. Leefbaar Gouda is also a strong supporter of referenda. Through a referendum, residents can make their voice heard on important decisions.

114745.explanation: More night permits will be issued for hospitality businesses so that there is more to do in the evenings.

114748.explanation: Within the framework of the dispersion law, we aim to phase out the asylum seekers center. In asylum reception, we choose realism. We will do what is necessary, but in our view, Gouda will not get a large and permanent asylum seekers center. Temporary reception remains temporary.

114752.explanation: From 2026, the fireworks ban applies. We believe initiatives at the neighborhood level should be supported regarding organized fireworks displays.

114757.explanation: RTV Gouwestad will receive assistance to improve quality. Picture and sound will be better and programs will have a more modern look. Broadcasts will soon be in full HD. It may also be necessary to employ paid staff.

114759.explanation: Creative and musical projects are encouraged because they contribute to the personal development of children and make education more attractive, but we see this as primarily the responsibility of the parents.

114763.explanation: The preference is for more police officers and BOAs on the street rather than placing cameras. But to support this, the use of cameras can be deployed.

114764.explanation: We support strengthening safety in our city. This requires good resources, clear tasks and safety in execution, also for the safety of the BOAs themselves.

114766.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. Therefore, they must be distributed fairly. Residents of Gouda receive priority based on the Housing Act. At least half of the rental homes allocated must go to Gouda residents.

114768.explanation: Housing must become affordable again so that starters, the elderly, and families can find a place in their own city. We will build 10,000 homes for Gouda residents, not for project developers.

114771.explanation: The reciprocal obligation tied to social assistance benefits should match the experience and capabilities of the individual and is not meant as harassment but to help someone move towards paid employment. Tasks performed in this context must not displace regular jobs.

114773.explanation: Existing support for residents with low incomes will remain, such as assistance through the Social Support Act (WMO) and the Long-Term Care Act (WLZ). No one should be left behind. But instead of raising the income threshold, people should have more money left in their pocket, for example by keeping municipal charges from increasing.

114778.explanation: In our opinion, every individual has the freedom to make their own choices, including in healthcare provision.

114779.explanation: Children should never be the victims of poor policy. Besides price, it is important to maintain high quality in care services.

114780.explanation: People should have more money left in their wallet, for example by preventing municipal charges from rising. Then municipal subsidies would not be necessary.

114781.explanation: Leefbaar Gouda keeps local taxes under control. Property tax must not increase between 2026 and 2030. If funds are needed for new policies, this must be solved within the existing budget.

114782.explanation: This is an outdated tax whose proceeds disappear into the general funds.

114784.explanation: In our opinion, the number of industrial estates should be reduced where possible to make room for housing.

114785.explanation: We want to leave the choice to shop or open stores on Sundays to residents and entrepreneurs themselves. Also, the regulatory burden on entrepreneurs must be reduced. Leefbaar Gouda finds it important that religion and politics always remain separate.

114786.explanation: Status holders will no longer receive priority for social rental housing and will wait their turn like everyone else. This ensures a fair distribution of housing and equal opportunities for all. Alternatives for status holders include flexible housing, shared housing, or temporary stays with family.

114788.explanation: The municipality does not decide what a person does on their private property.

114790.explanation: Leefbaar Gouda wants district teams to have more responsibility and their own budgets. This enables them to respond more quickly to specific neighborhood needs. A community center can be an initiative arising from such a district budget or the team's needs.

114792.explanation: As long as there are no rules to prevent nuisance and insufficient (police) supervision exists, Leefbaar Gouda opposes opening coffeeshops in Gouda's city center. Three coffeeshops should be removed from the inner city and relocated next to the police station.

114797.explanation: We strongly support offering the first hour of parking for free. After that, the tariff should remain in effect.

114798.explanation: When redesigning streets, Leefbaar Gouda believes parking spaces should not simply disappear. They are important for people dependent on cars, such as the elderly, families, and entrepreneurs. The municipality should invest more in additional parking spaces.

114799.explanation: Leefbaar Gouda states that parking spaces must not just disappear when streets are redesigned. We find these spots essential for those dependent on cars, such as the elderly, families, and entrepreneurs. Cars are here to stay, especially now that young people cannot leave home.

114800.explanation: Cargill still provides employment for a large number of our residents.

114801.explanation: The industrial estate must remain accessible for freight traffic.

114803.explanation: Such an investment leads to higher taxes and fees, and the passage is mainly intended for tourists.

114742.explanation: We give Gouda residents a democratic emergency brake in the form of a binding corrective referendum. This Gouda referendum can be requested and organized city-wide or at the neighborhood level.

114745.explanation: This causes arbitrariness and enforcement problems.

114748.explanation: This is not possible. The municipality has signed a long-term contract with the Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA). Terminating it unilaterally will cost millions.

114752.explanation: A dignified alternative to private fireworks.

114757.explanation: We support local media, so we know what happens in and around the city and politics is critically monitored.

114759.explanation: We make membership of (sports) clubs and access to culture free up to and including 12 years old.

114763.explanation: We want more police presence on the streets, not behind monitors.

114764.explanation: That is and remains a task for the police.

114766.explanation: We give maximum priority to Gouda residents in the allocation of social rental housing.

114768.explanation: A house is for living in, not for filling your pockets.

114771.explanation: Harassment measure. People are already elders/parents, (informal) caregivers, or doing other socially valuable work. An extra obligation adds nothing.

114773.explanation: We expand the target group for income support to 150 percent of the statutory social minimum.

114776.explanation: We lower the maximum speed limit to 30 kilometers per hour, except for arterial roads.

114778.explanation: A caregiver provides good care regardless of someone's background.

114781.explanation: We freeze municipal charges for Gouda residents.

114784.explanation: No room for it. Housing shortages take priority, and industrial estates can also be coordinated regionally.

114785.explanation: Sunday shopping is disadvantageous for small entrepreneurs compared to large chains.

114786.explanation: Urgent cases - including people going through a divorce or status holders - are not the cause of the housing shortage, so we do not make them scapegoats. We prefer to address the real problem of the housing shortage: the real estate sector.

114788.explanation: If a farmer wants to quit, we have no objection to that.

114790.explanation: A community center in every neighborhood.

114792.explanation: The current supply meets the demand.

114797.explanation: Current rates are sufficient; we will maintain them.

114798.explanation: There must be enough parking spaces in the neighborhoods for residents.

114799.explanation: The historic center of Gouda was not built for motorized traffic. Therefore, we will make the city center low-traffic; accessible only to residents, market vendors, local businesses, and-during delivery hours-suppliers.

114800.explanation: This is unrealistic in the short term.

114803.explanation: Where possible, we preserve that. Making additional waterways accessible to recreational boating is expensive and therefore not a priority.

114742.explanation: We believe that carefully introduced legislation cannot be replaced by a simple yes-no question. Volt considers it important that residents can participate early in the legislative process. We will create a permanent citizens' assembly where participants are randomly selected, informed, guided, and compensated.

114745.explanation: As far as Volt is concerned, hospitality entrepreneurs themselves are best placed to determine how they want to operate. This includes more flexible handling of opening hours. Volt welcomes more nightlife venues and meeting places for all different target groups in our city.

114748.explanation: Volt recognizes the importance of migrants and expats for Gouda and the need to accommodate asylum migrants. Gouda must provide enough places in the long term to house all asylum seekers in a humane way. This will reduce the need for expensive emergency shelters.

114752.explanation: Since 2026 there has been a ban on private fireworks, supported by Volt. As a replacement, we believe that centrally and responsibly organizing a fireworks show by the municipality is a good way to continue this festive tradition safely and in an environmentally responsible manner.

114757.explanation: Volt sees Radio and TV Gouwestad as indispensable for the local community. We want them to continue producing quality programs, hire more professional journalists, and receive a larger budget for live music, art, and culture. We also advocate for guaranteed airtime for local parties.

114759.explanation: Volt likes to invest in the creative development of (young) people and wants to explore how children from less affluent families can be financially supported. Volt sees music lessons as just one of many ways to express yourself creatively.

114763.explanation: With the government, safety and privacy come first. As in Estonia, we use the latest technology to keep personal data secure. Volt is therefore cautious about installing additional cameras in public spaces to safeguard citizens’ privacy.

114764.explanation: The monopoly on violence must remain with the police. At the same time, we advocate for strong cooperation between special enforcement officers (boa's) and the police. This offers the chance for a sustainably safer and more livable society.

114766.explanation: This rule can unintentionally impact people who have just found a job in Gouda. Therefore, we choose a non-discriminatory approach: social rental housing goes to those who need it most.

114768.explanation: We prefer owners to decide the purpose of their properties themselves but oppose speculators who raise house prices solely for personal gain with venture capital. Moreover, there is a need for additional mid-range rental homes in Gouda.

114771.explanation: Volt wants to introduce a basic income. Everyone receives a foundation to get ahead. This provides financial peace of mind and prevents debt. Additionally, social assistance benefits are a right you have in case of (long-term) illness and disability. Volt does not want to attach extra obligations to this.

114773.explanation: The social minimum must increase so that it is sufficient to guarantee subsistence security. It must at least be high enough to live on, have a roof over your head, and pay for things like healthcare costs, energy bills, phone, and internet.

114776.explanation: For the livability of the city, we will introduce a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h within built-up areas. We make a distinction between main roads where 50 km/h is still allowed and neighborhood streets where 30 km/h becomes the norm. 20 km/h is also an option on streets where safety is especially important.

114778.explanation: Volt values diversity in healthcare. For many people, it is difficult to discuss medical issues with caregivers of, for example, a different gender. Language barriers can also be a big problem. Volt also acknowledges that a diverse healthcare offer is not always practically possible.

114779.explanation: Volt is critical of market forces in youth care and advocates a structural improvement of youth care. Youth care becomes family care with the young person as the focus. This means broadly looking at what a family needs and that the involved young people have a say.

114780.explanation: Good healthcare is a basic right, not a luxury. Volt wants menstrual products to be available free of charge at schools, workplaces, healthcare institutions, and other public facilities. This guarantees that menstruation is not a barrier to education, work, or participation in society.

114781.explanation: With the increased housing prices in recent years, Volt believes the municipality should be cautious with property tax (ozb). Volt’s principle is for the municipality to work as efficiently as possible. Homeowners in Gouda must be able to afford this.

114782.explanation: Dogs are the only pets that require their own infrastructure. Think of dog walking areas, waste bags, and cleaning up dog poop in public. Therefore, Volt finds a dog tax justifiable.

114784.explanation: Volt wants Gouda to become an attractive breeding ground for innovative companies focused on manufacturing and skilled tradespeople.
